verdict: Worth doing, but with a SHARPLY narrowed target list. After reading source and tracing dynamic reachability, only 4 of the digest's top hotspots live on a path that real code executes: codec.ts:356 decodeEnvelopeCore (62/106), annotation-parser.ts:280 parse (58/75) + its helper splitTopLevel (17/36), mapper.ts:478 mapPresetType (41/66), and config/index.ts:120 validateConfig (26/39) + :259 loadConfigFile (19/33). The two highest-ranked entries after the codec function — analyzer.ts extractImports (27/92) and extractParameters (23/85) — live in src/core/analyzer.ts, which is DEAD (only non-test importer is the dead parallel-processor.ts). validation.ts:193 validateTypeHints (the medium-tier hotspot) is also dead by the same proof. Decomposing dead code is wasted effort and conflicts with the delete-dead-code theme, so I am explicitly excluding analyzer.ts and validation.ts. tywrap.ts:123 generate (38/91) is genuinely complex live code worth splitting; fetchPythonIr (25/27) is borderline and I scope it as optional. The codec, annotation-parser, and mapper functions are the genuine, high-leverage, behavior-preservable wins: each is a flat dispatch over a tagged discriminant (envelope marker / typing construct / preset name), which converts cleanly to a dispatch table with near-zero behavioral risk.
verifiedSignals: codec.ts:356 decodeEnvelopeCore is one mega-function dispatching on the string `marker` (__tywrap__) across 6 envelope types via a flat if-chain at lines 380/399/418/467/523/549, each block re-deriving `encoding`/`b64`/`shape` with near-identical guard prologues (verified 380-577) — cyclomatic 62 is real and is accidental complexity (repeated marker blocks), not irreducible logic

annotation-parser.ts:280 parse is a 220-line sequential if-chain of `raw.startsWith('typing.X[')` branches (Union 323, Optional 334, Literal 343, ClassVar/Final 356, Callable 390, Mapping 438, Annotated 452, Unpack 468), each repeating the `raw.slice(raw.indexOf('[')+1, raw.lastIndexOf(']'))` + splitTopLevel idiom (verified 280-497) — cognitive 75 is real, driven by branch count + the duplicated bracket-strip idiom

annotation-parser.ts:184 splitTopLevel is a genuine hand-rolled char scanner with quote/escape/bracket-depth state (verified 184-246); complexity 17/36 is largely IRREDUCIBLE state-machine logic, NOT a dispatch-table candidate

mapper.ts:478 mapPresetType is 6 near-identical `if (this.hasPreset('X')) { allowModule check; name===... returns }` blocks (stdlib 506, pandas 535, pydantic 552, scipy 559, torch 592, sklearn 607), verified 478-623 — cyclomatic 41 is accidental: a uniform (preset, moduleGuard, nameMap) shape repeated 6x

config/index.ts:259 loadConfigFile dispatches on file extension (.json 267, .cjs 277, .js/.mjs 285, .ts/.mts/.cts 291) with the .cts in-memory-compile and .mjs temp-file branches dominating cognitive load, verified 259-387 — the extension dispatch is extractable; the two eval strategies are the real complexity

config/index.ts:120 validateConfig is a flat sequence of independent field checks (top-level keys 131, output 137-155, runtime 157-167, performance 169-179, types/presets 181-204, debug 206), verified 120-209 — trivially splittable into per-section validators, cyclomatic 26 is purely additive

tywrap.ts:123 generate is ~220 lines mixing cache lookup (159-182), IR fetch, a 70-line inline export-filtering block (197-266) with a closured shouldExclude, file-emit/check-mode dual path (273-309), and unknown-type reporting (311-337), verified 123-345 — cognitive 91 is real and the export-filter block is a self-contained extractable unit
falsePositives: DIGEST/REVIEW target ranking implies analyzer.ts:561 extractImports (27/92) and :284 extractParameters (23/85) are top decomposition targets — REFUTED as actionable: src/core/analyzer.ts has ZERO non-test, non-dead importers. `grep -rln core/analyzer src/` returns only src/utils/parallel-processor.ts (line 15: `import { PyAnalyzer }`), which the dead-code theme deletes. analyzer is NOT exported from src/index.ts and NOT used by the generate() path (tywrap.ts uses transformIrToTsModel on Python IR JSON, not the TS tree-sitter analyzer). Decomposing it is wasted work; it should be DELETED by the other theme.

Digest medium-tier validation.ts:193 validateTypeHints (17/26) — REFUTED as actionable for the same reason: `grep -rln core/validation src/` returns zero non-test importers (only a comment match in index.ts:20). It is dead alongside analyzer; do not decompose.

splitTopLevel (17/36) being grouped with `parse` as a decomposition target could imply it is accidental complexity — PARTIALLY REFUTED: it is an irreducible char-by-char state machine (quote/escape/depth tracking). It should be LEFT mostly as-is; the only safe simplification is extracting the quote-state and bracket-depth transitions into tiny named helpers, which barely moves the metric. Do not over-engineer it.
proposal:
Codec (src/utils/codec.ts): Replace decodeEnvelopeCore's 6-branch if-chain with a dispatch table keyed by marker. Define a `type EnvelopeDecoder<T> = (value: Record<string, unknown>, ctx: DecodeCtx<T>) => MaybePromise<T|unknown>` and a `const ENVELOPE_DECODERS: Record<string, EnvelopeDecoder>` map with entries decodeDataframe, decodeSeries, decodeNdarray, decodeScipySparse, decodeTorchTensor, decodeSklearnEstimator. decodeEnvelopeCore shrinks to: guard isObject + marker-is-string, assertCodecVersion for the versioned set, look up `ENVELOPE_DECODERS[marker]`, return value unchanged if absent. Factor the repeated arrow-extraction prologue into `decodeArrowBacked(value, marker, decodeArrow): {bytes}` and the dataframe/series shared body (identical except the marker string in error messages) into one `decodeArrowOrJsonFrame(value, marker, decodeArrow)`. Keep decodeArrow/recurse passed via a small DecodeCtx object instead of positional params.

Annotation parser (src/core/annotation-parser.ts): Keep parse as the orchestrator but replace the long startsWith-chain with an ordered list of small rule functions: `type Rule = (raw: string, rawText: string, depth: number) => PythonType | null` evaluated in order, first non-null wins. Extract a `stripBracket(raw): string` helper (the repeated `raw.slice(raw.indexOf('[')+1, raw.lastIndexOf(']'))`) and a `prefixedMatch(raw, name): inner|null` helper (collapses the repeated `typing.X[ / typing_extensions.X[ / X[` triple). Rules: ruleTypeVarParam, ruleParamSpecArgs, ruleBuiltinClass, ruleUnionPipe, ruleUnion, ruleOptional, ruleLiteral, ruleExtractWrapper (ClassVar/Final/Required/NotRequired), ruleTypingFactory, ruleLiteralString, ruleCallable, ruleMapping, ruleAnnotated, ruleUnpack, ruleCollection, ruleGeneric, then mapSimpleName fallback. splitTopLevel stays as-is (irreducible scanner) aside from optionally naming its quote/bracket transitions.

Mapper (src/core/mapper.ts): Replace the 6 hasPreset blocks with a data-driven `PRESET_TYPE_RULES: ReadonlyArray<{preset, moduleGuard:(m?:string)=>boolean, build:(name, helpers)=>TypescriptType|undefined}>`. mapPresetType becomes: normalize, then `for (const rule of PRESET_TYPE_RULES) if (this.hasPreset(rule.preset) && rule.moduleGuard(moduleName)) { const t = rule.build(name, helpers); if (t) return t; }`. The shared `prop`/type literals and buildSparse move into a `helpers` object passed to build.

Config (src/config/index.ts): Split validateConfig into `validateTopLevelKeys`, `validateOutput`, `validateImportPath`, `validateRuntime`, `validatePerformance`, `validateTypes`, `validateDebug`; validateConfig calls them in sequence. Split loadConfigFile into an extension dispatch `LOADERS: Record<ext, (resolved, ext) => Promise<...>>` with `loadJsonConfig`, `loadCjsConfig`, `loadEsmConfig`, `loadTsConfig` (the .ts loader further delegates to `evalCtsInMemory` and `evalEsmViaTempFile`, which are the two genuinely complex sub-strategies).

generate() (src/tywrap.ts, OPTIONAL/scope-down): Extract the inline export-filtering block (lines 197-266) into `applyModuleExportFilters(moduleModel, moduleConfig, moduleKey, warnings)`, the cache read/write into `loadOrFetchIr(moduleKey, resolvedOptions, pythonPath, {checkMode, caching})`, and the file-emit/check dual path (273-309) into `emitOrCheckFiles(filesToEmit, {checkMode, written, outOfDate})`. The per-module loop body then reads as fetch -> transform -> filter -> generate -> emit.
breakingChanges: None intended for runtime behavior. All five decompositions are pure internal refactors of un-exported functions (decodeEnvelopeCore, parse, mapPresetType, the validateConfig section helpers, generate's internal blocks are all module-private). Public signatures decodeValue/decodeValueAsync, parseAnnotationToPythonType, TypeMapper.mapType, createConfig/resolveConfig/loadConfigFile, and generate() are unchanged.
loadConfigFile remains exported (src/config/index.ts:259) with identical signature; only its body is split — no break.

Risk-of-accidental-break to enumerate during review: error message strings in decodeEnvelopeCore (e.g. 'Invalid dataframe envelope: missing b64') are asserted by tests — the shared decodeArrowOrJsonFrame helper MUST interpolate the marker so messages stay byte-identical.
verificationPlan: Behavior-preservation is provable by existing tests: codec is covered by test/runtime_codec.test.ts, runtime_codec_scientific.test.ts, codec-performance.test.ts, safe-codec*.test.ts (these assert exact envelope error strings and round-trips for all 6 markers); annotation-parser and mapper are covered by their unit tests plus test-d type tests; config by config tests; generate() by tywrap/generate tests. Procedure: (1) run `npm test` + `npm run test:types` to capture a green baseline BEFORE touching anything; (2) refactor ONE function per commit; (3) re-run the targeted test file after each (e.g. `npx vitest run test/runtime_codec_scientific.test.ts`) — zero diffs in assertions proves preservation; (4) run full `npm run check:all` at the end. For the codec error-string risk, add/confirm a characterization assertion that each marker's malformed-envelope path still throws the exact message. To verify the complexity drop, re-run the same ts-fx scan that produced the digest: `ts-fx scan --check complexity --root /Users/brettbonner/tywrap --format ndjson` (the effective-config fact in complexity.ndjson shows the thresholds used: cognitiveThreshold 15, cyclomaticThreshold 10, maintainabilityThreshold 65) and diff the new NDJSON against /tmp/fx-tywrap/complexity.ndjson — confirm decodeEnvelopeCore/parse/mapPresetType/validateConfig/loadConfigFile no longer appear in the high-severity ranked list (each extracted helper should land under the per-function thresholds).
steps: Run npm test and npm run test:types to establish a green baseline; note the asserted codec error strings as the characterization contract.

codec.ts: extract decodeArrowOrJsonFrame(value, marker, decodeArrow) covering the identical dataframe+series bodies (preserving per-marker error strings), then extract per-marker decoder functions for ndarray/scipy.sparse/torch.tensor/sklearn.estimator, build ENVELOPE_DECODERS table, and reduce decodeEnvelopeCore to guard+assertCodecVersion+table lookup. Re-run codec test files.

annotation-parser.ts: add stripBracket and prefixedMatch helpers, convert the parse if-chain into an ordered Rule[] array evaluated first-non-null-wins, leaving splitTopLevel and the helper closures intact. Re-run parser unit + type tests.

mapper.ts: lift the per-preset blocks into a PRESET_TYPE_RULES data array with a shared helpers object (prop, type literals, buildSparse); rewrite mapPresetType as a loop over the rules. Re-run mapper tests.

config/index.ts: split validateConfig into per-section validators called in sequence; split loadConfigFile into an extension->loader dispatch with loadJsonConfig/loadCjsConfig/loadEsmConfig/loadTsConfig (and evalCtsInMemory/evalEsmViaTempFile inside the TS loader). Re-run config tests.

OPTIONAL: tywrap.ts generate() — extract applyModuleExportFilters, loadOrFetchIr, and emitOrCheckFiles; leave fetchPythonIr unless time permits a small extension of fetch-vs-fallback into a helper. Re-run generate tests.
Run npm run check:all (format, lint, build, type tests, unit tests) for the full regression gate.
Re-run `ts-fx scan --check complexity` with the digest's thresholds and diff the NDJSON to confirm the five functions dropped out of the high-severity list.
Do NOT touch src/core/analyzer.ts (extractImports/extractParameters) or src/core/validation.ts (validateTypeHints) — flag them to the delete-dead-code theme instead.
